title: Accessible Reader Surface, Untrusted Content, and Safe Links · Spiral Systems Review
summary: A delayed operations review reconstructs accessible reader surface, untrusted content, and safe links from revision, tenant, source URL, request validators, response bytes, parsed identity, transaction, lease, state transition, output, failure, cleanup, and recovery evidence. Planned artifact: Extend the evidence record for keyboard-first content-safe feed reader after a delayed changed case. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evi…
objectives: Treat titles, authors, summaries, HTML, images, categories, and URLs as untrusted publisher input through render-time safety boundaries.
Prefer safe text, use a tested allowlist sanitizer only where needed, rewrite links, block active content, and preserve readable structure.
Use one page heading, feed and entry landmarks, descriptive link text, metadata lists, dates, language, and source provenance.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Feed aggregation and Go Separate evidence from confidence · Review Feed Go Reflow Motion Media
steps.instruction:
review case — A review Go team handles case 4996 during an accessible community-news reader review. Build deterministic Go evidence to prove content-safe semantic reader structure, keyboard and focus behavior, reflow, and non-color evidence; failed, canceled, stale, and cross-tenant operations must preserve the prior admitted state; then change one RSS, Atom, namespace, date, identity, content, or encoding case and reconcile provenance. Browser Go uses pure bounded models and original RSS, Atom, HTTP, PostgreSQL, and reader fixtures only. Real DNS, sockets, XML libraries, PostgreSQL, processes, signals, race, load, containers, accessibility review, backups, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Support narrow reflow, zoom, reduced motion, non-color state, image alternatives, content warnings, and user-controlled media.

Inspect the Feed Go Reflow Motion Media trace for this review case. Which record connects a precondition, observed state change, and boundary result?

Evidence target: Feed aggregation and Go Separate evidence from confidence · Review Feed Go Reflow Motion Media.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for support narrow reflow, zoom, reduced motion, non-color state, image alternatives, content warnings, and user-controlled media. For Feed aggregation and Go Separate evidence from confidence · Review Feed Go Reflow Motion Media, keep Feed Go Reflow Motion Media connected to executable Go feed, XML, HTTP, PostgreSQL, schedule, API, reader, deployment, and recovery decision evidence.

lines.label: risk

lines.label: evidence

options.text: For separate evidence from confidence, import one convenient trusted RSS feed, omit reader and publisher outcomes, revision, discovery and SSRF scope, Atom and namespace cases, XML and HTTP budgets, PostgreSQL constraints and transaction, schedule, lease, goroutine cleanup, tenant, accessibility, recovery, and controlled native transfer, then accept plausible rows as feed-service proof.
The record names the assumption, applies Feed Go Reflow Motion Media, and reports a changed-case check tied to the stakeholder decision.
The record says the result looks right but supplies no quantity, trace, condition, or counterexample. That claim does not survive the separate evidence from confidence evidence boundary.

steps.title: Feed aggregation and Go Record what required effort · Review Feed Go Reflow Motion Media
steps.instruction:
Write the review evidence note for Feed Go Reflow Motion Media: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — A review Go team handles case 4805 during a multilingual university archive import. Build deterministic Go evidence to prove content-safe semantic reader structure, keyboard and focus behavior, reflow, and non-color evidence; browser Go must remain deterministic and free of network, database, process, and host effects; then interleave one schedule, lease, fencing, goroutine, cancellation, or shutdown path and prove ownership. Browser Go uses pure bounded models and original RSS, Atom, HTTP, PostgreSQL, and reader fixtures only. Real DNS, sockets, XML libraries, PostgreSQL, processes, signals, race, load, containers, accessibility review, backups, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Support narrow reflow, zoom, reduced motion, non-color state, image alternatives, content warnings, and user-controlled media.
